---
title: "MaAsLiN3 CANOE Workflow"
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: "2026-01-06"
output: html_document
---

#Learning MaAsLiN for differential abundance based on primary outcomes
```{r}
library(maaslin3)
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

# ===========================================================================
# 1.  EXTRACT DATA FROM PHYLOSEQ
# ===========================================================================
 
# Replace `my_physeq` with the name of your actual phyloseq object
physeq <- readRDS("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/phy_prev_10O_10R.rds")    # <-- change this
 
# -- ASV / feature table -----------------------------------------------------
#    MaAsLiN3 wants: rows = samples, columns = ASVs (features)
#    phyloseq stores OTU tables as features × samples or samples × features;
#    otu_table() + as.data.frame() handles both orientations automatically.
 
#feat_table <- as.data.frame(
#  t(otu_table(physeq))          # transpose so rows = samples (I believe mine are already row = samples)
#)
 
# If otu_table is already samples × features, skip the t():
feat_table <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq))
 
 
# -- Metadata ----------------------------------------------------------------
meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq))
meta <- as.data.frame(meta)
 

# -- Add sequencing depth to metadata ----------------------------------------
#    sample_sums() sums all reads (ASV counts) per sample.
#    This is the standard way to retrieve library size from a phyloseq object.
meta$sequencing_depth <- sample_sums(physeq)
 
 
# -- Quick sanity checks -----------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(all(rownames(feat_table) == rownames(meta)))   # samples must match
cat("Samples:", nrow(meta), "\n")
cat("ASVs   :", ncol(feat_table), "\n")
cat("Sequencing depth range:", range(meta$sequencing_depth), "\n")
 
 
# -- Rename columns to exactly what they are in YOUR metadata ----------------
#    The variable names below must match your sample_data() column names.
OUTCOME  <- "chronic_rhinitis36m"   # binary outcome of interest
SITE     <- "canoe_site.x"      # covariate 1 (categorical)
SEQDEPTH <- "sequencing_depth"        # covariate 2 (continuous, just added)
SUBJECT_ID <- "Subject_ID"
 
# Convert outcome to factor so MaAsLiN3 treats it as categorical
meta[[OUTCOME]] <- factor(meta[[OUTCOME]])
 
# Convert site to factor (if it isn't already)
meta[[SITE]] <- factor(meta[[SITE]])
 
meta <- data.frame(lapply(meta, function(x) {
  if (is.data.frame(x)) x[[1]] else x
}), row.names = rownames(meta))

# Double-check your key variables look right
str(meta[, c(OUTCOME, SITE, SEQDEPTH, SUBJECT_ID)])

# ===========================================================================
# 2.  RUN MaAsLiN3 — TSS + LOG (recommended default)
# ===========================================================================
#  Formula interpretation:
#    ~ chronic_rhinitis_36mo + sequencing_depth + site_of_collection
#    Tests the association of each ASV with the outcome while
#    controlling for sequencing depth and site.
#
#  normalization = "TSS"  →  relative abundance (proportions)
#  transform     = "LOG"  →  log2 of TSS-normalised proportions
#  augment       = TRUE   →  avoids linear separability in logistic models
#  standardize   = TRUE   →  z-scores continuous predictors for comparability
#  median_comparison_abundance = TRUE  →  computational compositionality correction
 
fit_TSS <- maaslin3(
  input_data     = feat_table,
  input_metadata = meta,
  output         = "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/",
  formula = paste0("~ ", outcome_var, " + ", SEQDEPTH, " + ", SITE,
                 " + (1|", SUBJECT_ID, ")"),
  normalization  = "TSS",
  transform      = "LOG",
  augment        = TRUE,
  standardize    = TRUE,
  max_significance          = 0.1,
  median_comparison_abundance  = TRUE,
  median_comparison_prevalence = FALSE,
  save_models    = FALSE
)
 

# ===========================================================================
# 3.  RUN MaAsLiN3 — CLR + NONE
# ===========================================================================
#  CLR (centred log-ratio) already incorporates a log transformation,
#  so transform must be set to "NONE" to avoid double-transforming.
#  Note: median_comparison_abundance is not needed / relevant with CLR
#        because CLR handles compositionality differently.
 
fit_CLR <- maaslin3(
  input_data     = feat_table,
  input_metadata = meta,
  output         = "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/maaslin3_output_CLR",
  formula = paste0("~ ", outcome_var, " + ", SEQDEPTH, " + ", SITE,
                 " + (1|", SUBJECT_ID, ")"),
  normalization  = "CLR",
  transform      = "NONE",    # <-- CRITICAL: do NOT use LOG with CLR
  augment        = TRUE,
  standardize    = TRUE,
  max_significance          = 0.1,
  median_comparison_abundance  = FALSE,
  median_comparison_prevalence = FALSE,
  warn_prevalence = FALSE,
  save_models    = FALSE
)
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 4.  EXTRACT RESULTS FOCUSED ON THE PRIMARY OUTCOME
# ===========================================================================
 
# MaAsLiN3 returns separate results for abundance and prevalence models.
# We filter to only rows where the metadata variable is our primary outcome.
 
extract_outcome_results <- function(fit_out, outcome_var, norm_label) {
 
  # Abundance model results
  abund <- fit_out$fit_data_abundance$results %>%
    filter(str_detect(metadata, fixed(outcome_var))) %>%
    mutate(model = "Abundance", norm = norm_label)
 
  # Prevalence model results
  prev  <- fit_out$fit_data_prevalence$results %>%
    filter(str_detect(metadata, fixed(outcome_var))) %>%
    mutate(model = "Prevalence", norm = norm_label)
 
  bind_rows(abund, prev)
}
 
res_TSS <- extract_outcome_results(fit_TSS, OUTCOME, "TSS + LOG")
res_CLR <- extract_outcome_results(fit_CLR, OUTCOME, "CLR")
 
# Combine into one data frame for comparative plotting
res_all <- bind_rows(res_TSS, res_CLR)
 
# Preview
head(res_all)
# Key columns:
#   feature    = ASV identifier
#   coef       = log-fold-change coefficient (effect size)
#   stderr     = standard error of the coefficient
#   pval       = raw p-value
#   qval       = BH-corrected q-value (FDR)
#   model      = "Abundance" or "Prevalence"
#   norm       = "TSS + LOG" or "CLR"
 
 
### Trying to think about why there are only TSS + LOG results for the res_all. ###
# Find ASVs significant in TSS but check their direction in CLR
res_TSS_sig <- res_TSS %>%
  filter(model == "Abundance", qval_individual < 0.1)

res_CLR_abund <- res_CLR %>%
  filter(model == "Abundance") %>%
  select(feature, coef_CLR = coef, pval_CLR = pval_individual, qval_CLR = qval_individual)

# Join and compare
comparison <- res_TSS_sig %>%
  left_join(res_CLR_abund, by = "feature") %>%
  mutate(
    concordant_direction = sign(coef) == sign(coef_CLR),
    CLR_nominal_sig      = pval_CLR < 0.05   # nominal, not FDR
  )

print(comparison)




# ===========================================================================
# 5.  VOLCANO PLOT FUNCTION
# ===========================================================================
 
make_volcano <- function(df,
                         title       = "Volcano Plot",
                         q_threshold = 0.1,
                         top_n_label = 15) {
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      neg_log10_q = -log10(qval_individual + 1e-10),
      direction   = case_when(
        qval_individual < q_threshold & coef > 0 ~ "Up (significant)",
        qval_individual < q_threshold & coef < 0 ~ "Down (significant)",
        TRUE                                      ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      direction = factor(direction,
                         levels = c("Up (significant)",
                                    "Down (significant)",
                                    "Not significant"))
    )

  top_hits <- df %>%
    filter(qval_individual < q_threshold) %>%
    arrange(qval_individual) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n_label)

  sig_line <- -log10(q_threshold)

  ggplot(df, aes(x = coef, y = neg_log10_q,
                 color = direction, label = feature)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = sig_line, linetype = "dashed",
               color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid",
               color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data         = top_hits,
      size         = 2.8,
      max.overlaps = 20,
      box.padding  = 0.4,
      segment.color = "grey50"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Up (significant)"   = "#D73027",
                 "Down (significant)" = "#4575B4",
                 "Not significant"    = "grey70"),
      name   = NULL
    ) +
    labs(
      title   = title,
      x       = "MaAsLiN3 coefficient (effect size)",
      y       = expression(-log[10](q-value[individual])),
      caption = paste0("Dashed line = q_individual < ", q_threshold, " (BH FDR)")
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
}
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 6.  BUILD AND SAVE VOLCANO PLOTS
# ===========================================================================
 
# ---- TSS + LOG: abundance --------------------------------------------------
v_TSS_abund <- res_TSS %>%
  filter(model == "Abundance") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "TSS + LOG  |  Abundance model\nChronic Rhinitis at 36 months")
 
# ---- TSS + LOG: prevalence -------------------------------------------------
v_TSS_prev <- res_TSS %>%
  filter(model == "Prevalence") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "TSS + LOG  |  Prevalence model\nChronic Rhinitis at 36 months")
 
# ---- CLR: abundance --------------------------------------------------------
v_CLR_abund <- res_CLR %>%
  filter(model == "Abundance") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "CLR  |  Abundance model\nChronic Rhinitis at 36 months")
 
# ---- CLR: prevalence -------------------------------------------------------
v_CLR_prev <- res_CLR %>%
  filter(model == "Prevalence") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "CLR  |  Prevalence model\nChronic Rhinitis at 36 months")
 
 
# ---- 2 × 2 composite figure ------------------------------------------------
composite_plot <- (v_TSS_abund | v_TSS_prev) /
                  (v_CLR_abund  | v_CLR_prev)  +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Differential Abundance: Chronic Rhinitis at 36 Months",
    subtitle = "MaAsLiN3 — comparing TSS+LOG vs CLR normalization",
    theme    = theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
                     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey40"))
  )
 
# Save to file
ggsave("maaslin3_volcano_composite.pdf",
       plot   = composite_plot,
       width  = 14, height = 11,
       device = "pdf")
 
ggsave("maaslin3_volcano_composite.png",
       plot  = composite_plot,
       width = 14, height = 11,
       dpi   = 300)
 
cat("Plots saved to working directory.\n")
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 7.  OPTIONAL — QUICK SUMMARY OF SIGNIFICANT HITS
# ===========================================================================
 
res_all %>%
  filter(qval_individual < 0.1) %>%
  select(norm, model, feature, coef, stderr, pval_individual, qval_individual) %>%
  arrange(norm, model, qval_individual) %>%
  print(n = 50)
 
 
###############################################################################
# QUICK REFERENCE: KEY MaAsLiN3 PARAMETERS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# normalization:  "TSS"  = total-sum scaling (proportions)      [recommended]
#                 "CLR"  = centred log-ratio (handles compositionality)
#                 "NONE" = use if data already normalised externally
#
# transform:      "LOG"  = log2 (use with TSS)                  [recommended]
#                 "NONE" = no transform (use with CLR — it already log-transforms)
#                 "PLOG" = pseudo-log (useful for metabolomics)
#
# median_comparison_abundance:
#                 TRUE   = subtract median coefficient across features
#                          (compositional correction — use with TSS)
#                 FALSE  = no median subtraction (use with CLR)
#
# augment:        TRUE   = adds small pseudo-observations to logistic model
#                          to avoid linear separability         [recommended]
#
# standardize:    TRUE   = z-score continuous covariates so coefficients
#                          are on the same scale                [recommended]
#
# max_significance: FDR (q-value) cutoff for "significant"     [default 0.1]
###############################################################################




```
